title: Topological property of graphene with triangular array of nanoholes
authors: Yong-Cheng Jiang, Xing-Xiang Wang, Xiao Hu
abstract: The nontrivial band topology for graphene with regular arrays of nanoholes with $C_{6v}$ symmetry is investigated theoretically. For the case of $3\sqrt{3} \times 3\sqrt{3}$ triangular array of nanoholes, we find an energy gap at $Γ$ point around the Fermi level associated with a band inversion which induces change in parity indices, whereas deep below the Fermi level there are a bunch of valence bands characterized as obstructed atomic limit (OAL) which also accommodate imbalance in parity indices. This band structure renders the gap at the Fermi level topologically trivial and carrying no edge states, while the nontrivial band topology of the OAL manifests in two flat bands in the ribbon structure associated with localized electronic states at ribbon edges. The present results exhibit rich topological behaviors in graphene derivatives waiting for explorations.
summary: This paper theoretically examines the band topology of graphene patterned with triangular nanoholes. Using a tight-binding model, the authors find that while the main gap at the Fermi level is topologically trivial, specific bands (OAL) retain nontrivial topology. This manifests as localized edge states in finite structures, suggesting a pathway for engineering topological behavior in graphene derivatives.
main_problem: The study investigates the nontrivial band topology of graphene when modified by regular arrays of nanoholes.
main_result: The energy gap at the Fermi level is found to be topologically trivial, but the Obstructed Atomic Limit (OAL) bands exhibit nontrivial topology, manifesting as localized edge states in ribbon structures.
method: Theoretical analysis using a spinless tight-binding (TB) model, diagnosing topology via parity index imbalance and Wilson loop phases.
model_or_system: The system is graphene with a $3\sqrt{3} 	imes 3\sqrt{3}$ triangular array of nanoholes, modeled using a tight-binding Hamiltonian for $\pi$ electrons.
key_observables: Energy gap at the Fermi level, parity index imbalance ($(N+M, N+\Gamma)$), and the presence/absence of edge states.
important_parameters: The specific geometry ($3\sqrt{3} 	imes 3\sqrt{3}$ array) and the Fermi level position are crucial.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on the TB model and assumes $C_{6v}$ symmetry; the appearance of edge states is linked to parity index imbalance in OAL bands.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the schematic structure, band structures showing parity indices and Wilson loops, and visualizations of the localized edge states in ribbon and flake geometries.
paper_structure: The paper proceeds by defining the physical system and Hamiltonian, applying topological diagnostics (parity indices, Wilson loops) to determine the nature of the gap, and finally analyzing the localized edge states arising from the OAL bands.
why_interesting: The work demonstrates how localized electronic states, arising from specific band features (OAL), can exhibit topological characteristics (edge states) even when the bulk gap is topologically trivial, providing insights into engineered topological materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
